返回日报
Hacker News #2

中国开放权重AI战略正在胜出

China’s open-weights AI strategy is winning

AI 作者 benwerd 热度 1178 回复 891 2026/07/20 22:21
打开原帖 打开外部链接 JSON

摘要

  • 中国采取开放权重AI战略，通过开源模型构建全球生态优势，而美国闭源策略面临挑战。
  • AI模型本身护城河有限，真正的壁垒在于企业服务和生态整合，开放模型更易被采用和创新。
  • 美国出口管制和闭源策略可能削弱其AI竞争力，需调整政策以支持开放技术发展。

看点

  • 中国开放权重AI模型在成本和性能上逐渐逼近美国闭源模型。
  • 开放模型在基础设施采用中更具优势，支持无许可创新和灵活部署。
  • 美国AI公司因闭源策略可能失去全球生态主导权。
  • 80%的初创公司可能在使用中国AI模型，显示其市场渗透力。
  • 文章呼吁美国调整政策，支持开放AI技术以符合公共利益。

文章深入分析了中国开放权重AI战略与美国闭源AI战略的对比，涉及AI模型、开源生态、算力限制、企业服务等科技与AI核心议题。

正文翻译

暂无正文

暂无原文

回复翻译 20

geophile 2026/07/21 02:21
过去 50 年计算机和软件市场的教训是免费和低端最终获胜。

- PC 摧毁了小型计算机。大型机得以生存，但所服务的市场份额比以前要小得多。

- PC 办公生产力软件摧毁了昂贵的专业产品。

- Windows（低端）和 Linux（免费）彻底摧毁了 UNIX 市场，并再次从大型机世界夺取了巨大的市场份额。

暂时忽略巨大的中国开放重量模型：

- 前沿模型的培训成本和资源需求是不可持续的。高价格和社会阻力意味着生产这些模型的美国公司岌岌可危。

- 研究成果有巨大的经济激励，可以生产出更便宜、资源密集程度较低的高质量模型。

- 消费者硬件方面的本地法学硕士类似于 70 年代和 80 年代的 PC 爱好者世界。

将所有这些趋势放在一起，我认为在 10-15 年内，我们消费者 PC（和手机！）运行的模型几乎可以完成前沿模型目前可以做的任何事情。

回到中国模型：它们允许与 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 进行新的竞争，基本上 SaaS 以更便宜的价格出租这些功能强大的 AI。这只会加速趋势。

The lesson of the last 50 years of the computer and software marketplace is that free and low-end eventually wins.

- PCs destroyed minicomputers. Mainframes survive, but serving a much tinier portion of the market than they used to.

- PC office productivity software destroyed expensive professional products.

- Windows (low end) and Linux (free) completely destroyed the UNIX marketplace, and again, have taken huge market share from the mainframe world.

Ignoring the huge Chinese open-weight models for a moment:

- The training costs and resource requirements for frontier models are unsustainable. The high price, and social pushback, mean that the American companies producing these models are precarious.

- There are enormous financial incentives for research results allowing for cheaper, less resource-intensive models of high quality.

- Local LLMs on consumer hardware are akin to the PC hobbyist world of the 70s and 80s.

Put all of these trends together, and I think that in 10-15 years, we are going to have consumer PCs (and phones!) running models doing pretty much anything that frontier models can do right now.

Getting back to the Chinese models: They allow for new competition against Anthropic and OpenAI, basically SaaS renting out these very capable AIs much cheaper. That will just accelerate trends.

楼层
gpt5 2026/07/21 02:43
现在的问题是，开放权重模型现在依赖于大公司花费数十亿美元来训练和开发它们，所有这些都得到了它们的激励措施和国家的支持，同时基本上放弃了它们的货币化路径。

通过开源项目，好处是每个人都可以相互改进复杂的系统（例如 Linux 内核），并且随着时间的推移，收益会累积。对于目前的模型，根本没有办法进行分布式训练，或者实际上没有任何大规模并行方法来改进它们。

因此，无论推动公开模型权重的短期策略是什么（例如，可能会制造价格战，以向西方公司施加压力并剥夺他们所需的资金），我们都不能忽视这样一个事实，即激励和决策在未来很容易发生变化，除非有一种方法可以真正分散模型改进 - 该党可以在任何情况下停止时间。

The problem (right now) is that Open Weight models depend right now on huge companies to spend billion of dollars to train and develop them, all backed up by their incentives and their state to support this, while essentially giving away their monetization path.

With open source projects, the benefit was that each individual could improve the complex system (e.g. Linux Kernel) interpedently, and over time the benefits accumulated. With models right now, there is just no way to do distributed training, or really, any large scale parallel way to improve them.

So whatever the short term strategy driving publicizing the model weights (e.g. potentially, to create a price war in order to put pressure on western companies and deprive them of the money they need), we can't ignore the fact that incentives and decisions could easily change in the future, and unless there is a way to truly decentralize models improvements - the party could stop at any time.

楼层
geophile 2026/07/21 03:18
但是不同的大公司有不同的激励措施。让我在他们卖给我的 4000 美元机器上运行强大的开源模型非常符合 Nvidia 的利益。
But different huge companies have different incentives. It is very much in Nvidia’s interest to have me running a powerful open source model on a $4k machine that they sell me.
楼层
nightpool 2026/07/21 05:21
是吗？当他们能让你在他们成批卖给企业消费者的5万美元一台的机器上运行更强大的模型时？我们已经看到内存制造商放弃低端市场转而支持服务器。我不清楚英伟达是否认为个人GPU是他们比出售数百万台服务器农场级机器更好的长期投资
Is it? When they could be having you running an even more powerful model on a $50k machine they sell by the pallet-load to enterprise consumers? We already see RAM manufacturers abandoning the low-end market in favor of server support. It's not clear to me that Nvidia sees personal GPUs as their best long term investment compared to selling millions of server-farm class machines
楼层
layla5alive 2026/07/21 06:06
你是说50万美元一台的机器，还是1500万美元一个的机架……与仅仅十年前相比，成本已经变得难以想象地巨大了。
You mean a $500k machine, or a $15M rack... the costs have gotten unimaginably large from the lens of just a decade ago.
楼层
anonzzzies 2026/07/21 19:49
...对于人工智能...人们必须说服自己他们“需要”。我是一名用户，我的小公司有一个价值 100 万美元的 AI 机架，但我目前有 189 个机架，每个机架的成本约为 15k。这些技术比人工智能技术给我们带来了更多的钱；大多数是部门应用程序或网站，它们只需要很少的处理能力，并且一切都很低，而人们却花很多钱来维持它们的运行。我们的支持比其他人更好；你可以打电话给我或者来我家，我们会做点什么。从来没有多少客户离开。过去30年。我购买了 5-10 年的服务器，这对于我 99% 的客户（有些是财富 500 强公司，但属于部门级的公司）来说完全是多余的，而且它们永远不会离开。赚的钱比人工智能多得多，而且我们的价格仍然比 AWS 便宜，而且该部门不需要与我们一起获得灵活的预算（就像使用 AWS 一样）。我们只有相同的固定价格+年度通胀修正，仅此而已。 30 年。
... for AI... which people have to convince themselves they 'need'. I am a user, I have a 1m$ rack for my small company for AI, but I have currently 189 racks that cost about 15k one off per rack. And those make us a LOT more money than the AI ones ; most are departmental apps or websites that take very little processing power and are low on everything while people pay nice money to keep them going. Our support is better than the rest; you can just call me or come to my house and we'll do something. Not many clients leave, ever. For the past 30 years. I buy 5-10 year old servers which are complete overkill for 99% of my clients (some are fortune 500 companies but departmental stuff) and they just never leave. Makes vastly more money than AI and we are still cheaper than AWS and the department does not have to get a flexible budget (like they would have to if AWS) with us. We just have the same fixed price + yearly inflation correction and that's it. For 30 years.
楼层
davnicwil 2026/07/21 06:28
向个人销售可能是一项更加稳健、可预测的业务，按托盘销售的问题是收入激增，如果大客户停止购买，收入也会迅速下降。另一个问题是销售谈判压低了大宗买家的利润等。消费类硬件在很多方面都是一个非常有吸引力的市场，看看苹果就知道了。
Selling to individuals can be a hugely more robust predictable business, the problem with selling by the pallet load is spiky revenue that can also quickly fall off a cliff if larger customers stop buying. The other problem is sales negotiations driving down margins for bulk buyers etc. Consumer hardware is a very attractive market in lots of ways, just look at Apple.
楼层
leoc 2026/07/21 07:01
对。区分硬件制造商在供应受到限制和不受限制时的激励因素可能很重要。 （我不是任何方面的专家。）只要供应受到严格限制，他们自然就会卖给最高出价者，即大型 LLM SaaS 参与者。不过，如果供应不再受到限制，情况就会大不相同：

1) LLM SaaS 公司是硬件提供商垂直分解的一种形式，是承担成本并从客户向硬件提供商提供的资金中获取利润的中间人。如果不再有好的模型可供本地使用而硬件人员无需支付任何费用，情况就会有所改变，但只是有所改变

2) LLM SaaS 公司是其硬件资源的高效用户。虽然供应受到限制，但这有助于使它们成为最高竞标者，从而对硬件制造商有吸引力的客户。当供应不受限制时，这种情况应该会逆转。对于硬件制造商来说，哪一类客户更有吸引力：公司里都是高学历的人，他们整天都在努力减少资源使用，还是让笔记本电脑平均每天闲置约 18 小时？

例如，值得注意的是，nVidia 继续重点关注 AI 紧凑型台式机和笔记本电脑。毫无疑问，这在一定程度上是为了更好的开发商关系和良好的公关，但英伟达也可能着眼于退出，并为未来从向军队销售铲子到在沃尔玛销售铲子的转型做准备。但当然，未来并不明确。如果硬件制造商，尤其是 RAM 制造商，从现在开始就没有足够的未来产能，那么我们可能会在相当长的一段时间内陷入供应紧张的困境。 （进一步）政府行动可能会影响事物等等。如果前沿实验室很快找到新的方法来使用更多的内存、GPU容量等，并且不会对抗收益递减，那么他们可能会在一段时间内保持王者地位，尽管现在看来不太可能。

Right. It's probably important to distinguish what the hardware manufacturers' incentives are when their supply is constrained and when it isn't. (I am not an expert on anything.) As long as supply is strongly constrained they're naturally going to sell to the highest bidders, which are the big LLM SaaS players. If and when supply is no longer constrained, though, things will look very different:

1) The LLM SaaS companies are a form of vertical disintegration for the hardware providers, a middleman covering costs and taking profits out of the money that comes from customers to the hardware providers. That changes somewhat if there are no longer good models available for local use at no cost to the hardware guys, but only somewhat

2) The LLM SaaS companies are efficient users of their hardware resources. While supply is constrained this helps to make them top bidders and so attractive customers for the hardware manufacturers. When supply is not constrained this should reverse. Which is the more attractive class of customer to a hardware maker: the company full of people with higher degrees who spend their whole working day fighting to pare back resource usage, or the guy who leaves his laptop idle about 18 hours per day on average?

It's notable that nVidia, for instance, has continued to put significant emphasis on AI compact desktops and laptops. And while no doubt that's partly in the service of better developer relations and good PR in general, it's probably also nVidia eyeing the exit, and preparing for a future transition from selling shovels to the army to selling shovels at Walmart. But of course the future isn't clear and obvious. If the hardware makers, maybe the RAM guys in particular, turn out to have underbuilt future capacity starting in the present then we could be stuck in constrained supply for quite a long time. (Futher) government action could affect things etc. etc. And if the frontier labs soon find new ways to use still larger amounts of memory, GPU capacity etc. that isn't butting up against diminishing returns then they'll likely remain kings for some time, though that does not seem probable now.

They might not release such a thing, lest it puts all of their customers out of business.

MetroWind 2026/07/22 00:28
不，在中国根本不是这样。中国的每个人都在使用人工智能，甚至老人和孩子。 DeepSeek 已经开始赚钱了。
Nope, that's not true at all in China. Everybody in China is using AI, even the elderlies and the kids. DeepSeek is already earning money.
楼层
DrScientist 2026/07/21 18:01
谁资助了大部分尖端科学研究？

是公司还是政府？

如果世界各国政府都认为基于公共知识建立的法学硕士与公共知识本身一样具有竞争性，那么他们为什么不持续资助它呢？

Who funds the majority of cutting edge scientific research?

Is it companies or is it governments?

If governments around the world see LLMs built from public knowledge as pre-competitive as the public knowledge itself, then why wouldn't they sustainbly fund it?

楼层
stanzeo 2026/07/21 22:31
因为政府的商业模式很难做。收入主要来自税收，所以你必须能够不断解释为什么继续在这项资金上花费预算是个好主意。
Because the business model for governments is difficult. Income are mostly taxes, so you have to be able to explain constantly why it is a good idea to keep on spending budget on that funding.
楼层
mbgerring 2026/07/22 01:29
如果人工智能乐观主义者是正确的，并且人工智能可以全面提高生产力，那么税收收入就会增加。不是火箭科学。
If AI optimists are correct, and AI can increase productivity across the board, tax revenues go up. Not rocket science.
楼层
rapind 2026/07/22 00:47
我构建它的方式就像太空竞赛（除了可能更重要）。中国正在与他们的行业合作并鼓励他们的行业，绝对倾向于国家资助的美国宇航局路线，而美国则完全接受造币寡头，他们很可能会在以后纾困。

我们会看到会发生什么，但我怀疑历史会告诉我们，美国在最初的领先优势上严重失误。中国可能刚刚踩下了油门。

The way I frame it is like the space race (except probably more consequential). China is collaborating with and encouraging their industry, definitely leaning into the state sponsored NASA route, while the United States fully embraced minting oligarchs that they'll most likely bail out later.

We'll see what happens, but I suspect history will show us that the United States badly fumbled what was a pretty great initial lead. China might have just floored the gas pedal.

楼层
woctordho 2026/07/21 19:15
需要付出很多个人努力来改进模型。看看 Hugging Face 上有多少经过微调的模型和 LoRA。
There's a lot of individual effort of improving the models. See how many finetuned models and LoRAs are there on Hugging Face.
楼层
shiftingleft 2026/07/21 19:19
在资源需求方面，微调模型与训练整个模型有很大的不同。
fine-tuning a model is very different from training the whole model in terms of resource requirements.
楼层
21asdffdsa12 2026/07/21 20:02
苏？如果需要更好的版本，全世界可以汇集资源，组建一个公司来训练这个模型，然后这个公司倒闭，模型又重新开源。
Soo? If they need a better version, the world can pool resources together, form a company that trains the model, then the company goes under and the model becomes open source again.
楼层
dinfinity 2026/07/21 03:08
> 目前的问题在于，开放权重模型现在依赖于大型公司花费数十亿美元来训练和开发它们，这一切都由它们的利益驱动和国家支持作为后盾，同时基本上放弃了它们的变现途径。

想象一下用这种方式来进行基础科学研究。“唉，这赚不到钱，所以就不会发生。”

社会不仅仅是资本主义。

> The problem (right now) is that Open Weight models depend right now on huge companies to spend billion of dollars to train and develop them, all backed up by their incentives and their state to support this, while essentially giving away their monetization path.

Imagine approaching fundamental scientific research like that. "Welp, it can't make money, so it won't happen."

There is more to society than capitalism.

楼层
jonahx 2026/07/21 03:13
> 想象一下用这种方式来进行基础科学研究。“唉，这赚不到钱，所以就不会发生。”

> 社会不仅仅是资本主义。

我不认为GP的意思是那样。我把它理解为“我们应该认识到对于重要成果存在不稳定的激励情况，并开始考虑其他解决方案。”

> Imagine approaching fundamental scientific research like that. "Welp, it can't make money, so it won't happen."

> There is more to society than capitalism.

I don't read GP like that. I read it as "we should recognize a situation of unstable incentives for an important outcome, and start thinking about other solutions."

thayne 2026/07/21 09:57
嗯，这多少就是文章的主旨。为了“获胜”，美国需要一种鼓励开放模型的激励结构。

不过我不确定那会是什么样子的。

Well that's kind of the point of the article. That in order to "win", the US needs an incentive structure that encourages open models.

I'm not sure what that looks like though.

楼层
